text_original: Motse said: To make any statement or to publisli any doctrine, there must first be established some standard of judgment To discuss without a standard is like determining the directions of sunrise and sunset on a revolving potter’s wheel.
text_normalized: Motse said: To make any statement or to publisli any doctrine, there must first be established some standard of judgment To discuss without a standard is like determining the directions of sunrise and sunset on a revolving potter’s wheel.

text_original: Even skilful artisans could not get accurate results in that way.
text_normalized: Even skilful artisans could not get accurate results in that way.

text_original: Xow that the truth and error (of a doctrine) in the world is hard to tell, there must be three tests.
text_normalized: Xow that the truth and error (of a doctrine) in the world is hard to tell, there must be three tests.

text_original: What are the three tests?
text_normalized: What are the three tests?

text_original: They are the test of its basis, the test of its verifiability, and the test of its applicability.
text_normalized: They are the test of its basis, the test of its verifiability, and the test of its applicability.

text_original: To test the basis of a doctrine we shall examine the will of Heaven and spirits and the deeds of the sage-kings.
text_normalized: To test the basis of a doctrine we shall examine the will of Heaven and spirits and the deeds of the sage-kings.

text_original: To test its verifiability we shall go to the books of the early kings.
text_normalized: To test its verifiability we shall go to the books of the early kings.

text_original: As to its applicability it is to be tested by its use in the administration of justice and government.
text_normalized: As to its applicability it is to be tested by its use in the administration of justice and government.

text_original: These then are the three tests of a doctrine.
text_normalized: These then are the three tests of a doctrine.

text_original: Among the gentlemen of to-day some think there is fate, some think there is no fate.
text_normalized: Among the gentlemen of to-day some think there is fate, some think there is no fate.

text_original: That I am able to judge whether there is fate or not is by the sense testimony of the multitude.
text_normalized: That I am able to judge whether there is fate or not is by the sense testimony of the multitude.

text_original: If some have heard it and some have seen it I shall say there is fate.
text_normalized: If some have heard it and some have seen it I shall say there is fate.

text_original: If none has heard it, if none has seen it, I shall say there is no fate.
text_normalized: If none has heard it, if none has seen it, I shall say there is no fate.

text_original: Why not then let us inquire into the sense testimony of the people?
text_normalized: Why not then let us inquire into the sense testimony of the people?

text_original: From antiquity to the present, since the beginning of man, has any seen such a thing as fate, or has heard the sound of fate?
text_normalized: From antiquity to the present, since the beginning of man, has any seen such a thing as fate, or has heard the sound of fate?

text_original: Of course, there is none.
text_normalized: Of course, there is none.

text_original: If the common people are considered stupid and their senses of hearing and sight unreliable, then why not inquire into til© recorded statements of the feudal lords?
text_normalized: If the common people are considered stupid and their senses of hearing and sight unreliable, then why not inquire into til© recorded statements of the feudal lords?

text_original: But from antiquity to the present, since the beginning of m has any of them heard the sound of fate or seen such a thing as fate?
text_normalized: But from antiquity to the present, since the beginning of m has any of them heard the sound of fate or seen such a thing as fate?

text_original: Of course, none of them has.
text_normalized: Of course, none of them has.

text_original: Again, why not let us inquire into the deeds of the sage-kings?
text_normalized: Again, why not let us inquire into the deeds of the sage-kings?

text_original: The ancient kings promoted the filial sons and encouraged them to continue to serve their parents, and respected the virtuous and gentle and encouraged them to continue to do good.
text_normalized: The ancient kings promoted the filial sons and encouraged them to continue to serve their parents, and respected the virtuous and gentle and encouraged them to continue to do good.

text_original: They published their orders to instruct (the people), and made reward and pmiishment fair to encourage (the good) and obstruct (the evil).
text_normalized: They published their orders to instruct (the people), and made reward and pmiishment fair to encourage (the good) and obstruct (the evil).

text_original: In this way confusion could be reduced to order and danger could be converted to peace.
text_normalized: In this way confusion could be reduced to order and danger could be converted to peace.

text_original: If anyone doubts this, let us recall; In ancient times the confusion of Chieh was reduced to order by T’ang, and that of Chow by King Wu.
text_normalized: If anyone doubts this, let us recall; In ancient times the confusion of Chieh was reduced to order by T’ang, and that of Chow by King Wu.

text_original: Now, the times did not change and the people did not alter.
text_normalized: Now, the times did not change and the people did not alter.

text_original: Yet when the superior changed a regime the subordinates modified their conduct.
text_normalized: Yet when the superior changed a regime the subordinates modified their conduct.

text_original: Under T’ang and Wu it was orderly, but under Chieh and Chow it was disorderly.
text_normalized: Under T’ang and Wu it was orderly, but under Chieh and Chow it was disorderly.

text_original: Hence peace and danger, order and disorder, all depend on the government of the superior.
text_normalized: Hence peace and danger, order and disorder, all depend on the government of the superior.

text_original: How can it be said everything is according to fate?
text_normalized: How can it be said everything is according to fate?

text_original: So, assertions about there being fate are quite false.
text_normalized: So, assertions about there being fate are quite false.

text_original: The fatalists tell us: This doctrine has not been invented by us in a late generation.
text_normalized: The fatalists tell us: This doctrine has not been invented by us in a late generation.

text_original: Such a doctrine has appeared and been handed down since the Three Dynasties.
text_normalized: Such a doctrine has appeared and been handed down since the Three Dynasties.

text_original: Why do you, sir, now oppose it?
text_normalized: Why do you, sir, now oppose it?

text_original: " (In answer,) Motse asked: Was it from the sages and good men of the Three Dynasties or from the wicked and the vicious of the Three Dynasties that the fatalistic doctrine came?
text_normalized: " (In answer,) Motse asked: Was it from the sages and good men of the Three Dynasties or from the wicked and the vicious of the Three Dynasties that the fatalistic doctrine came?

text_original: How can we find this out?
text_normalized: How can we find this out?

text_original: In the beginning secretaries and ministers were careful in speecli and intelligent in conduct.
text_normalized: In the beginning secretaries and ministers were careful in speecli and intelligent in conduct.

text_original: They could persuade their ruler above and instruct the people below.
text_normalized: They could persuade their ruler above and instruct the people below.

text_original: Thus they obtained reward from their ruler and applause from the people.
text_normalized: Thus they obtained reward from their ruler and applause from the people.

text_original: And the fame of those secretaries and ministers has come down to the present day.
text_normalized: And the fame of those secretaries and ministers has come down to the present day.

text_original: The whole world remarks: " This is the result of endeavour." And it will never say; I see fate there." On the other hand, the wicked kings of the Three Dynasties did not control the lust of their ears and eyes and did not restrain the passions of their heart.
text_normalized: The whole world remarks: " This is the result of endeavour." And it will never say; I see fate there." On the other hand, the wicked kings of the Three Dynasties did not control the lust of their ears and eyes and did not restrain the passions of their heart.

text_original: When they went out they indulged in racing, hunting, and trapping.
text_normalized: When they went out they indulged in racing, hunting, and trapping.

text_original: When they stayed indoors they revelled in wine and music.
text_normalized: When they stayed indoors they revelled in wine and music.

text_original: They did not attend to the government of the country and of the people, but they did much that was of no use.
text_normalized: They did not attend to the government of the country and of the people, but they did much that was of no use.

text_original: They oppressed the people, causing the subordinates not to love their superior.
text_normalized: They oppressed the people, causing the subordinates not to love their superior.

text_original: Hence the country became empty and without any future, and they themselves were in punishment and disaster.
text_normalized: Hence the country became empty and without any future, and they themselves were in punishment and disaster.

text_original: But they would not confess and say: I am stupid and insolent and poor in administering the government." But they would say: It is but my fate to perish." Even the miserable people of the Three Dynasties were like this.
text_normalized: But they would not confess and say: I am stupid and insolent and poor in administering the government." But they would say: It is but my fate to perish." Even the miserable people of the Three Dynasties were like this.

text_original: Within they could not well serve their parents, with-out they could not well serve their ruler.
text_normalized: Within they could not well serve their parents, with-out they could not well serve their ruler.

text_original: They disliked politeness and frugality but liked licence and ease.
text_normalized: They disliked politeness and frugality but liked licence and ease.

text_original: They indulged in drinking and eating and were lazy.
text_normalized: They indulged in drinking and eating and were lazy.

text_original: The means of food and clothing became insufficient and they placed themselves in danger of hunger and cold.
text_normalized: The means of food and clothing became insufficient and they placed themselves in danger of hunger and cold.

text_original: They would not confess: I am stupid and insolent and was not diligent at work." But they would say: It is but my fate to be poor." Such, then, also were the miserable people of the Three Dynasties.
text_normalized: They would not confess: I am stupid and insolent and was not diligent at work." But they would say: It is but my fate to be poor." Such, then, also were the miserable people of the Three Dynasties.

text_original: Fatalism has been glossed over and taught the stupid people.
text_normalized: Fatalism has been glossed over and taught the stupid people.

text_original: This was of great concern to the sage-kings, and they put it down on the bamboos and silk and cut it in metals and stone.
text_normalized: This was of great concern to the sage-kings, and they put it down on the bamboos and silk and cut it in metals and stone.

text_original: Among the books of the kings, "The Announcement of Chung Hui" says: I have heard the man of Hsia issue orders, pretending them to be fate of Heaven.
text_normalized: Among the books of the kings, "The Announcement of Chung Hui" says: I have heard the man of Hsia issue orders, pretending them to be fate of Heaven.

text_original: God was displeased and destroyed his forces." This shows how King Chieh of Hsia believed; in fate and how both T’ang and Chung Hui thought it to be ong.
text_normalized: God was displeased and destroyed his forces." This shows how King Chieh of Hsia believed; in fate and how both T’ang and Chung Hui thought it to be ong.

text_original: Among the books of early kings " The Great Declaration" says: 'Chow became insolent and would not worship God, and pushed away the ancestors and spirits without offering them sacrifices.
text_normalized: Among the books of early kings " The Great Declaration" says: 'Chow became insolent and would not worship God, and pushed away the ancestors and spirits without offering them sacrifices.

text_original: And he said: ‘Fortune is with my people,’ and neglected and betrayed his duty.
text_normalized: And he said: ‘Fortune is with my people,’ and neglected and betrayed his duty.

text_original: Heaven thereupon ‘.deserted him and withdrew its protection." This shows how Chow believed in fate and how King Wu proclaimed it to be wrong with " The Great Declaration Again, not come from Heaven, but is shaped by one’s self." And it is said in the odes and books of Shang and Hsia: Fate ■ is born of the TO | So, then, if the gentlemen of the world desire to distinguish right and wrong, benefit and harm, fate of Heaven must be strenuously discredited.
text_normalized: Heaven thereupon ‘.deserted him and withdrew its protection." This shows how Chow believed in fate and how King Wu proclaimed it to be wrong with " The Great Declaration Again, not come from Heaven, but is shaped by one’s self." And it is said in the odes and books of Shang and Hsia: Fate ■ is born of the TO | So, then, if the gentlemen of the world desire to distinguish right and wrong, benefit and harm, fate of Heaven must be strenuously discredited.

text_original: To hold there is fate is the great disaster of the world.
text_normalized: To hold there is fate is the great disaster of the world.

text_original: And therefore Motse refuted it.
text_normalized: And therefore Motse refuted it.
